author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tava pada⋅paṅkajinī, jīvāmṛta⋅sañcāriṇī,
atibhāgye jīva tāhā pāya
se⋅amṛta pāna kari’, mugdha haya tāhe hari,
āra tāhā chāḍite nā cāya

niviṣṭa hoiyā tāya, anya sthāne nāhi jāya,
anya rasa tuccha kari’ māne
madhupūrṇa padmasthita, madhuvrata kadācita,
nāhi cāya ikṣudaṇḍa⋅pāne

e bhaktivinoda kabe, se⋅paṅkajasthita ha’be,
nāhi jā’be saṁsārābhimukhe
bhaktakṛpā, bhaktibala, ei duiṭī susambala,
pāile se⋅sthiti ghaṭe sukhe
en_translation:
O Hari! A living being obtains Your lotus feet, which distribute the nectar of life, only by great fortune. Drinking that nectar, the soul becomes enchanted and never wishes to leave.

Becoming absorbed there, one never goes anywhere else, considering all other tastes as insignificant. A bee residing in a lotus full of honey never desires sugarcane.

When will Bhaktivinoda become situated in those lotus feet and never again turn toward the material world? That state occurs happily when the mercy of devotees and the strength of devotion are received.
